description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a textured, shingle-like surface and a small brick chimney at the ridge. The exterior walls are finished in a half-timbered style, with dark wooden beams set against light plaster panels, evoking a traditional Tudor or medieval European aesthetic. The front facade includes a central wooden door with vertical planks and a round handle, flanked by timber framing and lower wooden paneling. The side elevations display prominent diagonal and vertical wooden beams, with no visible windows, emphasizing the structural timberwork. The roof overhangs the walls slightly, providing some shelter to the entryway and sides. The overall structure is compact, single-story, and appears to be constructed primarily from wood and plaster, with a rustic, hand-crafted appearance.
description: A compact, single-story house with a steep gable roof, half-timbered walls, and a central wooden door. The design is rustic and reminiscent of traditional Tudor or medieval European cottages.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden door, half-timbered facade, and the shingled gable roof with a chimney.
description_view_1: Side gable view highlighting the triangular wall with prominent timber cross-bracing and no visible windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with timber framing and no entry or window features, plus the full expanse of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side gable view, similar to view 1, with symmetrical timber cross-bracing and the chimney visible at the roof peak.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden plank door with a round handle, framed by timber beams and lower wood paneling, no porch or steps.
